name: Qailertetang
description: Qailertetang, also known as Qailertaq, is a powerful and complex deity within Inuit mythology, specifically revered in Labrador. She is primarily recognized as the goddess of animals, particularly caribou, and is responsible for the well-being and abundance of these vital creatures. Qailertetang's role is crucial for the survival of the Inuit people, as caribou provide essential resources such as food, clothing, and tools. She controls the release of caribou from their mythical home, a hidden place known only to her, and can either grant or withhold them based on the respect and adherence to traditional customs shown by the Inuit. It is believed that disrespectful behavior towards animals or the breaking of taboos can anger Qailertetang, resulting in scarcity and hardship. Offerings and prayers are traditionally made to her to ensure a successful hunt and the continued prosperity of the community. She is seen as a guardian of the natural world and a regulator of the balance between humans and animals. Her power extends beyond caribou, encompassing a general responsibility for the welfare of animals and the success of hunting endeavors. Qailertetang is often depicted as a stern and demanding figure, emphasizing the importance of respect and responsibility in the relationship between humans and the environment. The Inuit understand that their survival is intrinsically linked to maintaining a harmonious relationship with Qailertetang and the animal world she governs. She embodies the delicate balance and the consequences of disrupting that balance.
subtitle: Goddess of Animals, Caribou Mother

keyMyths.title: The Release of the Caribou
keyMyths.description: Qailertetang controls the release of caribou from their hidden home, ensuring the Inuit have the resources they need. Disrespecting animals can cause her to withhold the caribou, leading to starvation.

keyMyths.title: The Taboo of Broken Bones
keyMyths.description: It is said that breaking the bones of caribou is disrespectful to Qailertetang and will lead to a poor hunt. This taboo emphasizes the need to respect the animal even after it has been hunted.

keyMyths.title: Offerings to Qailertetang
keyMyths.description: Inuit hunters traditionally make offerings to Qailertetang before and after a hunt to ensure success and to show gratitude for the animals provided. These offerings can include tobacco or small pieces of meat.

sources.author: Franz Boas
